title: Eyes Open
brief: Your first MS command. The Sentry radar advertises an AMTI capability — but you don't point a sensor, you schedule it. Submit an AMTI_CommandMT against the advertised CapabilityID with a valid time window, then watch the activity confirm and the tracks come in.
teaches: Sensors are scheduled, not pointed (MS Vol §1.2.8). Reference the advertised CapabilityID from MA_AMTI_CapabilityMT, give a time window that hasn't passed, and wait: AMTI_CommandStatusMT RECEIVED→ACCEPTED, AMTI_ActivityMT ENABLED→ACTIVE→COMPLETED, then EntityMT tracks. MS isn't safety-critical — a command with an unadvertised capability isn't REJECTED, it's just quietly CANCELED and nothing collects (lie #20).

objective._doc: No flight. Win = two sensor tracks (EntityMT) collected. A valid AMTI_CommandMT (NEW, advertised CapabilityID, window not passed) schedules the activity; it goes ACTIVE at tick 4 and reports TRK-1 at t5 and TRK-2 at t7. A command with a wrong CapabilityID is CANCELED and never collects.
